title: Managed audit manual sandbox connection credential resolver human approval artifact review upstream echo verification

sourceNodeV307.sourceSpan: Node v306 + Java v142 + mini-kv v135

requiredFields.label: Artifact id
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Stable artifact id bound to the human review packet.

requiredFields.label: Operator approval reference
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Human approval reference or ticket handle.

requiredFields.label: Credential handle review status
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Review status for the credential handle, not credential value.

requiredFields.label: Endpoint handle allowlist review status
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Allowlist review status for endpoint handle, not raw URL.

requiredFields.label: No-network safety test reference
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Evidence that review packet validation performs no HTTP/TCP request.

requiredFields.label: Manual abort semantics reference
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Documented operator abort behavior before runtime discussion.

requiredFields.label: Rollback semantics reference
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Documented rollback behavior without executing rollback.

requiredFields.label: Created by operator identity
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Verified operator identity for the review artifact.

requiredFields.label: Audit correlation id
requiredFields.requiredEvidence: Correlation id linking artifact review, audit trail, and later echo verification.

prohibitedFields.reason: Credential values must never enter Node, Java, or mini-kv evidence.
The review packet may carry endpoint handle status, not raw endpoint URLs.
Provider config would move v308 from contract review into implementation.
Resolver client config would instantiate the runtime path too early.
v308 must not prepare or send HTTP/TCP payloads.
Ledger writes are outside this read-only packet contract.
Schema migration SQL is prohibited in the review packet.
mini-kv remains non-authoritative and must not receive write/admin commands.
Runtime shell invocation is still blocked after v308.

rejectionReasons.message: Reject when artifact_id is absent or blank.
Reject when the operator approval reference is absent.
Reject when credential handle review status is absent.
Reject when endpoint handle allowlist review status is absent.
Reject when no-network safety test reference is absent.
Reject when abort or rollback semantics are missing.
Reject any artifact that includes credential values.
Reject any artifact that includes a raw endpoint URL.
Reject provider or resolver client config.
Reject external request payloads or execution requests.
Reject ledger writes and schema migration requests.
Reject mini-kv writes, admin commands, and authority claims.
Reject runtime shell implementation or invocation requests.

noGoBoundaries.reason: Review only endpoint handle allowlist status.
No secret provider can be instantiated in v308.
No resolver client can be instantiated in v308.
No fake provider/client is introduced as a shortcut.
No HTTP/TCP request is prepared or sent.
Disabled runtime shell implementation remains blocked.
mini-kv stays a read-only evidence provider.
Node must not start Java, mini-kv, or external audit services.

echoVerification.sourceSpan: Node v308 + Java v143 + mini-kv v136

warnings.message: v309 proves Java v143 and mini-kv v136 echoed the Node v308 packet; it still does not approve or implement a runtime shell.

recommendations.message: The next Node step may consume the v309 receipt, but it must still reject credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client config, HTTP/TCP calls, ledger writes, schema migration, mini-kv writes/admin commands, and automatic upstream start.
Do not convert upstream echo alignment into implementation permission; require a separate future packet and explicit approval gates.

nextActions: Archive Node v309 as the human approval artifact review upstream echo verification that consumes Node v308, Java v143, and mini-kv v136.
Keep disabled runtime shell implementation and invocation blocked after v309; this version only proves the packet contract was echoed upstream.

The next plan may consider a future Node-only read-only preflight, but it must still reject credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client config, HTTP/TCP calls, ledger writes, schema migration, mini-kv writes/admin commands, and automatic upstream start.
